evals.name: scope-inference-from-path
evals.prompt: I just edited src/templates/00-blank/Composition.tsx to add a fade-in animation on the title text. Please write the git commit message for this change.
evals.expected_output: A Conventional Commits message of the form `feat(00-blank): <single English sentence>`. Type should be `feat`. Scope should be inferred as `00-blank` from the file path. Description should be one concise English imperative sentence describing the fade-in animation.

evals.name: chinese-trigger-reject-wip
evals.prompt: 幫我 commit 這個 wip 的修改：我改了 src/Root.tsx，重新命名了 import 路徑。
evals.expected_output: Skill should trigger despite Chinese phrasing. Output should reject 'wip' and produce a proper Conventional Commits message — likely `refactor: <description>` or `refactor(root): <description>` describing the rename. Description should be single English sentence, imperative mood.

evals.name: improve-type-user-visible-polish
evals.prompt: 我把表單送出後那個「成功」提示，從原本一出現就定住，改成淡入、停一下、再淡出，讓現有的提示順眼一點。沒有加新功能，也沒有修 bug。幫我寫 commit message。
evals.expected_output: Type should be `improve` — this refines the look-and-feel of an EXISTING user-facing element (the success toast): user-visible polish, not a new feature even though it adds a fade (it polishes existing behaviour rather than adding a capability). Must NOT pick `feat`, `style` (not code formatting), or `fix` (nothing was broken). e.g. `improve: fade the success toast in and out`. Single English imperative sentence, triggers despite Chinese phrasing.

evals.name: style-not-refactor-for-formatting
evals.prompt: I reflowed the whitespace and added missing semicolons across src/parser.ts — no logic changed at all. Write the commit message.
evals.expected_output: Type should be `style` (code formatting only: whitespace, semicolons). Must NOT be `refactor` (no logic or structure changed), `feat`, or `fix`. e.g. `style: reflow whitespace and add semicolons in parser`. Guards the style-vs-refactor trap called out in the SKILL anti-patterns.

evals.name: breaking-change-footer
evals.prompt: I renamed the public Config.setOutputLocation method to Config.setOutputPath and removed the old name entirely. Write the commit message.
evals.expected_output: Subject like `refactor(config): rename setOutputLocation to setOutputPath`, AND a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer noting the old name is removed and to migrate to setOutputPath. The assertion is that a breaking public-API change MUST emit the BREAKING CHANGE footer (one concise sentence), not just a subject line.

evals.name: reject-vague-update
evals.prompt: commit 一下，我更新了一些東西。
evals.expected_output: Should reject the vague 'update X' / non-specific framing (a SKILL anti-pattern) rather than emitting a `chore: update stuff`-style message. Should infer or ask for a concrete type plus a specific description of what actually changed. Triggers despite Chinese phrasing.
